title: Image path allows environment variable
body:
### Feature request description

It would be nice to allow the use of environment variables in the `Image` path, within the `Container` section.

```
[Container]
Image=my.internal.repo/service-a:{ENV_VERSION}
```

```
[Container]
Image=my.internal.repo/service-b:{ENV_VERSION}
```

when referencing an environment file, we would just need to edit that file, and not all `*.container` files

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I've tried adding `EnvironmentFile` to both `Service` and `Container` section.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
That should work if you specify the env under the [Service] section. The [Container] section env is just added as env into the container so it will not affect the unit itself.
https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/25053

oh, I'm really sorry , I read your linked issue, and explanation made totally sense, and it works as expected. I just didn't read the error message correctly. I just had a bad value for my variable. again, really sorry for your time. thanks!
